commit.message:
test: add proptest-based fuzzing harnesses for all HID protocol crates (#164)

Add property-based test harnesses to ensure malformed HID reports
never cause panics, undefined behavior, or safety violations across
all supported device vendors.

Crates with new proptest harnesses:
- hid-fanatec-protocol: standard, extended, pedal parsers
- hid-simucube-protocol: HID report, input report, steering bounds
- hid-asetek-protocol: parse never panics, derived finite, truncation
- hid-cammus-protocol: parse never panics, axes bounded, truncation
- hid-openffboard-protocol: parse, steering, button safety, truncation
- hid-ffbeast-protocol: parse, parse_with_id, normalized, wrong ID
- hid-heusinkveld-protocol: parse, normalized finite, truncation
- hid-button-box-protocol: gamepad, extended, axis, button, truncation
- hid-moza-protocol: HBP, SRP, any PID, axes bounded
- hid-thrustmaster-protocol: enhanced with parse_input, parse_pedal,
  axes_bounded, truncation tests

Cross-vendor integration test:
- New hid_cross_vendor_fuzz_tests.rs feeds random bytes to all 14+
  parser functions simultaneously, including edge cases (empty, single
  byte, all-0xFF, all-0x00, max HID report size)

commit.message:
fix(main): guard main() call to prevent execution during test imports

When tests import `parseArgs` from main.ts, the top-level `main()` call
also executes. In CI (no `gh auth`), this triggers `process.exit(1)`,
causing vitest to report an unhandled rejection and fail the run.

Add an entry-point check using `import.meta.url` so `main()` only runs
when the file is executed directly.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

test: add comprehensive proptest harnesses for Forza and AMS2 telemetry (#163)

Add property-based fuzz tests covering:

telemetry-forza (new proptest_fuzz.rs, 18 tests):
- Random bytes no-panic for all 4 packet formats (Sled/CarDash/FM8/FH4)
- Valid sled invariants (speed, RPM, velocity magnitude)
- CarDash round-trip (throttle/brake/gear preservation)
- Race-off zeroed telemetry verification
- Truncated packet rejection (below Sled size)
- Inter-format size rejection (between known sizes)
- Off-by-one boundary conditions
- Oversized packet rejection
- NaN/Infinity extreme values
- Deterministic normalization

telemetry-ams2 (expanded proptest_fuzz.rs, 14 tests):
- Invariant verification on valid parsed output
- Controls round-trip (throttle/brake/clutch)
- Truncated buffer rejection
- Off-by-one boundary conditions
- Oversized buffer parsing
- NaN/Infinity/NEG_INFINITY handling
- Zero fuel capacity division guard
- G-force finite invariants
- Tire data non-negative invariants
- Deterministic normalization

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
test: add device protocol parsing snapshot tests for Fanatec, Simagic, VRS, and Moza (#162)

Add comprehensive snapshot/golden-file tests for input report parsing
across all four HID protocol crates using insta. Tests cover:

- Fanatec: standard/extended/pedal report parsing, inverted pedal axes,
  12-bit Hall sensor masking, protocol version detection, VID/PID
  category matrix, rim/wheelbase/pedal capability matrices (16 tests)
- Simagic: firmware version detection from byte offsets, all gear
  positions, shifter flag combinations, half-press pedal axes, quick
  release states, model-from-PID matrix, vendor ID constants (9 tests)
- VRS: connection detection (all-FF steering), quarter-range values,
  button bitmask parsing, signed encoder values, vendor ID, full device
  identity matrix (7 tests)
- Moza: wheelbase aggregated input report parsing, pedal axes extraction,
  minimal report threshold, V1/V2 PID pattern verification, device
  identity for all known PIDs, model torque matrix, rim ID constants,
  ES compatibility matrix (11 tests)

All tests use Result-returning signatures (no unwrap/expect) per project
conventions. Snapshot files are committed alongside test sources.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat(modules): add perf-auditor agent — chrome-devtools-mcp + Lighthouse + CWV

New perf-auditor module (category: quality, builtin: true, ON by default).

Files:
- platform/tools/perf_audit_tools.py — 7 BaseTool subclasses:
  perf_audit_lighthouse, perf_trace_start, perf_trace_stop,
  perf_analyze_insight, perf_network_slow, perf_console_errors,
  perf_emulate_mobile
- skills/perf-audit.md — 3 audit workflows + output format + perf budgets
- platform/mcps/store.py — mcp-chrome-devtools entry (npx chrome-devtools-mcp)
- platform/agents/tool_runner.py — cdp in mcp_id_map + perf-auditor guard
- platform/modules/registry.yaml — perf-auditor module (24 total)

Architecture: two coexisting browser MCPs:
  mcp-playwright     = automation (click/fill/navigate/test)
  mcp-chrome-devtools = diagnostics (Lighthouse/CWV/traces/network)

Source: https://github.com/ChromeDevTools/chrome-devtools-mcp (Apache-2.0)
Rationale: playwright-mcp has no Lighthouse, CDP traces, or CWV.
This fills the gap: same tooling as Chrome DevTools, wired into TMA auto-heal.

Fix B20+B21: dependent items/rules master_itemid and item prototype params

B20 - Dependent discovery rules and items now capture and restore master_item_key:
- Add master_item_key field to DiscoveryRule and Item models
- Export: _raw_to_template resolves master_itemid→key for items and discovery rules
- Export: _item_dict and _rule_dict serialize master_item_key when set

- Deployer: _create_discovery_rule accepts item_key_to_id, sets master_itemid
  for dependent discovery rules
- Deployer: _handle_discovery_rule_change fetches existing items from Zabbix
  for ADD case to resolve master_itemid

- Add params field to ItemPrototype model
- get_item_prototypes: add params and master_itemid to output fields
- Export: _raw_to_template sets params and master_item_key on item prototypes
- Export: _proto_dict serializes params and master_item_key when set
- Deployer: _create_item_prototype sends params when non-empty
- Export: item prototypes build proto_itemid_to_key map per rule for
  resolving master_item_key on dependent prototypes

Full restore test passed:
- zbx export 'zbx-restore-test' → YAML with all fields including params,
  master_item_key for items/rules/prototypes
- zbx apply → restored all 4 items (calculated+dependent+regular),

commit.message:
feat(tls_certificate): add `mittwald_tls_certificate` resource (#343)

Wildcard TLS certificates must be provisioned separately via the
mittwald API since they require DNS validation and cannot be implicitly
created with a virtual host. This adds a `mittwald_tls_certificate`
resource to manage that lifecycle. Additionally, the resource supports
importing pre-existing PEM-encoded certificates and private keys.

## Changes

- **New resource `mittwald_tls_certificate`**
(`internal/provider/resource/tlscertificateresource/`)
  - Supports two creation modes:
- **DNS validation**: provide `common_name` + `project_id` to request a
DNS-validated certificate (suitable for wildcard domains like
`*.foobar.example`)
- **Certificate import**: provide `certificate`, `private_key_wo`,
`private_key_wo_version`, and optionally `common_name` to import a
pre-existing PEM-encoded certificate
  - Create blocks (up to 30 min) with a two-phase polling strategy:
    1. Polls `ssl-get-certificate-request` until `isCompleted=true`
2. Finds the issued certificate via `ssl-list-certificates` by matching
`certificateRequestId`
3. For DNS-validated certificates, additionally polls
`ssl-get-certificate` until `dnsCertSpec.status.status == "ready"`;
terminal error states (`error`, `cnameError`) are surfaced immediately
- Imported certificates (no `DnsCertSpec`) skip the readiness polling
step
- Both `certificate_request_id` and `id` (certificate) are persisted in
state
- `common_name` is Optional+Computed — required for DNS validation,
auto-derived from the certificate CN for imports
- `private_key_wo` is a write-only attribute; the private key is re-sent
to the API when `private_key_wo_version` changes or `certificate`
changes
- In-place certificate renewal via `ReplaceCertificate` when
`certificate` or `private_key_wo_version` changes
- Switching between DNS validation and certificate import modes forces
replacement
  - Delete cleans up both the certificate and the certificate request
  - Supports `terraform import` by certificate ID
- **Config validation**: `certificate`, `private_key_wo`, and
`private_key_wo_version` must all be set together or not at all;
`common_name` is required when none of the import fields are set (DNS
validation mode)

- **Registered** the new resource in `provider.go`

- **Examples**:
- `examples/resources/mittwald_tls_certificate/resource.tf` — DNS
validation (wildcard cert + `mittwald_virtualhost` with `depends_on`)
- `examples/resources/mittwald_tls_certificate/resource_import.tf` —
certificate import from PEM files with `private_key_wo`

- **README** updated to include `mittwald_tls_certificate` in the
resources list

- **Docs** regenerated via `go generate`
(`docs/resources/tls_certificate.md`)

## Usage

### DNS validation (wildcard)

```hcl
resource "mittwald_tls_certificate" "wildcard" {
  project_id  = var.project_id
  common_name = "*.foobar.example"
}

resource "mittwald_virtualhost" "example" {
  project_id = var.project_id
  hostname   = "app.foobar.example"
  paths      = { "/" = {} }

  depends_on = [mittwald_tls_certificate.wildcard]
}
```

### Certificate import

```hcl
resource "mittwald_tls_certificate" "imported" {
  project_id = var.project_id

  certificate            = file("${path.module}/cert.pem")
  private_key_wo         = file("${path.module}/key.pem")
  private_key_wo_version = 1
}

resource "mittwald_virtualhost" "example" {
  project_id = var.project_id
  hostname   = "foo.example"
  paths      = { "/" = {} }

  depends_on = [mittwald_tls_certificate.imported]
}
```

commit.message:
fix: make S3 bucket SSM read graceful before terraform apply

The thumbnail-bucket-name SSM parameter doesn't exist yet because the
S3/IRSA terraform resources haven't been applied. Hard-failing the SSM
read breaks CI for every deploy until terraform apply runs.

Read the parameter with a 2>/dev/null fallback to empty string. Only
inject the S3 helm args (aws.bucketName, aws.region, serviceAccount.roleArn)
when the bucket is provisioned. When empty the API falls back to the
existing data URI path — same behaviour as before the S3 work.

Once terraform apply runs for the S3 resources the SSM param will exist
and the next CI run automatically picks up S3 storage with no further
changes needed.

feat: add mount command with transparent FSAPI bridge

Adds a `mount` shell command that uses the File System Access API to
create a transparent bidirectional bridge between a real local directory
and a VirtualFS path. No copying occurs — reads and writes flow directly
through the FileSystemDirectoryHandle.

Changes:
- src/fs/mount-commands.ts (new): MountCommands class that opens the
  directory picker (readwrite mode) and calls vfs.mount(). Supports
  `mount list` and `mount unmount <path>` sub-commands.
- src/fs/virtual-fs.ts: added mount()/unmount()/listMounts() methods and
  a private findMount() helper. All filesystem ops (readFile, writeFile,
  readDir, mkdir, rm, stat, exists) check for active mount points first
  and delegate to the FSAPI handle instead of LightningFS.
- src/shell/wasm-shell.ts: registers MountCommands and wires up the
  `mount` custom command alongside the existing `git` command.

Works on http://localhost (secure context) without HTTPS.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

test(ipc): add comprehensive IPC versioning and compat tests (#161)

Add 57 tests in ipc_versioning_compat_tests.rs covering:

- Version mismatch matrix: systematic client/server version pair testing
  with exhaustive compatibility checks (16 version pairs)
- Wire format golden-byte snapshots: pinned byte-level header encoding
  to catch silent wire format changes
- Backward compatibility: legacy (field-missing) protobuf messages
  decoded by current schemas with correct defaults
- Unknown field handling: future protobuf fields silently ignored,
  unknown message types/flags preserved through roundtrip
- Transport fallback: TCP always available, platform-default differs,
  builder can switch to TCP, serde roundtrip for persistence
- Timeout and retry behavior: error classification (recoverable vs
  fatal), structured error fields, server lifecycle edge cases
- Feature negotiation lifecycle: full connect/negotiate/disconnect,
  degraded mode with zero matching features, manual registration
- Concurrent version negotiation: 10 parallel negotiations, mixed
  compatible/incompatible clients, concurrent register/unregister
- Wire format evolution: cross-version encode/decode (legacy->current,
  current->future, legacy->future), mixed-version message streams,
  codec size validation

All tests use Result-returning style (no unwrap/expect).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Add comprehensive adaptive scheduling tests (#160)

Add 37 tests covering all aspects of the adaptive scheduling system:

- Load detection: EMA processing-time tracking (5 tests)
- Rate transitions: period increases under load, decreases when healthy
- Safety constraints: period never exceeds min/max bounds
- Hysteresis: dead-zone prevents oscillation between rates
- Device capability: narrow/wide period ranges respected
- Recovery: full return to base rate after load spike
- Budget tracking: processing time recording and enforcement
- Configuration: validation, normalization, builder pattern
- State snapshots: fraction calculations, boundary checks
- Edge cases: equal bounds, reconfiguration, disable/enable

All tests use Result-returning signatures (no unwrap/expect).
Tests with wait_for_tick use 5ms periods for CI stability and
high jitter thresholds to isolate processing-time signals.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
